claims.text: Large language models struggle to catch errors in their own outputs when the review happens in the same session that produced them.
claims.source_quote: Large language models struggle to catch errors in their own outputs when the review happens in the same session that produced them.

claims.text: Cross-Context Review (CCR) is a method where the review is conducted in a fresh session with no access to the production conversation history.
claims.source_quote: This paper introduces Cross-Context Review (CCR), a straightforward method where the review is conducted in a fresh session with no access to the production conversation history.

claims.text: A controlled experiment was conducted with 30 artifacts and 150 injected errors.
claims.source_quote: We ran a controlled experiment: 30 artifacts (code, technical documents, presentation scripts) with 150 injected errors, tested under four review conditions...

claims.text: Cross-Context Review (CCR) reached an F1 of 28.6% over 360 reviews.
claims.source_quote: Over 360 reviews, CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%...

claims.text: Cross-Context Review (CCR) outperformed Self-Review (SR) with an F1 of 24.6%.
claims.source_quote: CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%, outperforming SR (24.6%, p=0.008, d=0.52)...

claims.text: Cross-Context Review (CCR) outperformed repeated Self-Review (SR2) with an F1 of 21.7%.
claims.source_quote: CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%, outperforming... SR2 (21.7%, p<0.001, d=0.72)...

claims.text: Cross-Context Review (CCR) outperformed context-aware Subagent Review (SA) with an F1 of 23.8%.
claims.source_quote: CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%, outperforming... SA (23.8%, p=0.004, d=0.57).

claims.text: Reviewing twice in the same session did not beat reviewing once.
claims.source_quote: The SR2 result matters most for interpretation: reviewing twice in the same session did not beat reviewing once (p=0.11)...

claims.text: The benefit of Cross-Context Review (CCR) comes from context separation itself.
claims.source_quote: The benefit comes from context separation itself.

claims.text: Cross-Context Review (CCR) works with any model.
claims.source_quote: CCR works with any model, needs no infrastructure, and costs only one extra session.
CCR works with any model, needs no infrastructure, and costs only one extra session.

claims.text: Cross-Context Review (CCR) costs only one extra session.
claims.source_quote: CCR works with any model, needs no infrastructure, and costs only one extra session.
